Definition.text: Buff is an Effect that modifies statistics and is identified by a green icon border.

Purpose.text: Represent attached positive effects and include non-damaging side effects such as degeneration when defined by the buff source.

Core Rules.text: A Buff adds modifiers to the attached creature; Buff itself cannot deliver hits or actions. Debuffs are the detriment-side effect class.

Internal Rules.text: Charges and flask effects are not Buff effects; Buff duration is affected by Buff duration and, where applicable, Skill Effect Duration.

Calculation Order.text: 1) effect instance attached 2) modifier aggregation by copy/strongest stacking rules 3) duration tracking 4) periodic expiry and replacement handling.

Priority Rules.text: Multiple copies of the same buff do not stack; only the strongest buff is active unless explicitly marked as a stacking buff or exception.

Preconditions.text: Valid source effect and valid target attachment state are required; icon visibility is a UI trait, not a behavioral requirement.

Postconditions.text: Target receives active buff/debuff modifiers and expiration behavior according to copy and duration rules.

Limitations.text: Buffs on enemies are generally not exposed on player UI; some effect types are exception-handled for visibility and timing.

Caps.text: Copy caps apply through strongest-rule semantics; exceptions (e.g., Withered stacks) may define explicit stack limits.

Formula.text: No single universal formula for all Buffs; each Buff defines its own modifier terms and aggregation is rule-driven by copy strength or stack exception.

cannot_affect: charge or flask mechanics classified outside Buff

Conflicts.text: Buff effect modifier scaling and curse/debuff aura application have changed across patches and can conflict with legacy semantics.

Exceptions.text: Stacking buffs are exceptions to strongest-only rule; some systems explicitly allow multiple instances up to limits.

Hidden Rules.text: Aura-derived and support-driven Buff applications may use modifier pipelines not obvious from icon-only inspection.

Developer Notes.text: Treat Buff as a general effect class with explicit Debuff separation and exception-based stacking behavior.

Historical Changes.text: Patch history indicates targeted fixes to aura/buff transition, inversion bugs, and aura effect scope for enemy auras.

text.note: Some buff effects now transition with the player through combat areas.
Most buff effect modifiers can no longer invert the effect of buffs.
Increased Effect of Auras on You modifiers no longer apply to enemy debuff auras.

text: Wrath applies a lightning damage buff effect through an aura.
Multiple same-name buffs do not stack unless explicitly exceptional.
A red-border effect is not necessarily a Buff but usually a Debuff.
All positive effects are not guaranteed to be Buffs when represented by icons or names.

prevents: inversion by most buff effect modifiers
cannot_affect: flask and charge systems classified outside buff effect model
